purpose: Map the expected timing sequence of commodity-inflation transmission for monitoring and positioning, while acknowledging that specific timing windows are imprecise.
components: Tier 1 — Energy direct (1-3 months): gasoline, heating, electricity
Tier 2 — Food and goods (3-12 months): farm-gate to retail food, manufactured goods, energy-food circular dependence via fertilizer
Tier 3 — Services and wages (6-36 months): services ex-shelter, rent/OER, wage negotiations
Tier 4 — Corporate hedging rolloff: predictable second-wave cost mechanism as hedges expire
Firm-level validation: equity_analyst_02's three-tier decomposition (0-1, 2-4, 4-8 quarters)
Warning: 90% CI for energy-to-CPI spans 1-14 months — use as ordering framework, not timing tool
current_assessment: The tiered structure is well-established as a qualitative ordering. The 2021-23 episode provides the clearest modern template: goods peaked Q1 2022, services ex-shelter Q3 2022, shelter Q1 2023. However, specific timing is too imprecise for most investment decision horizons.
limitations: Specific timing windows carry wide confidence intervals (90% CI: 1-14 months for energy-to-CPI)
Timing varies significantly by episode in ways that preclude precise forecasting
Corporate hedging rolloff timing depends on firm-specific hedge tenors
Passthrough illusion hypothesis suggests tiered lags may mimic second-round breadth, confusing diagnosis
